text: Does the article fit in the universe of sustainability analyses we seek to assess? (Yes / No)
What problems or set of problems is the article trying to address?
Do the analysis, conclusions, and theories derived from, and directed to, understanding and/or managing a clearly specified "on the ground" problem or class of problems? (Yes / No)
Provide arguments that supports your response to Q3 (on the ground problem context). Plus, cite some key text from the article that supports your response.
Are the analysis, conclusions, and theories generated to apply beyond understanding, and/or managing a clearly specified “on the ground” problem or class of problems? (Yes / No)
Provide arguments that supports your response to Q5 (beyond on the ground problem context). Plus, cite some key text from the article that supports your response.

Do the analysis, conclusions, and theories treat individuals, organizations and states as largely self-interested, satisfaction driven entities that seek to maximize some kind “utility” outcome? (Yes / No)
Provide arguments that supports your response to Q7 (self-interested agents). Plus, cite some key text from the article that supports your response.

Do the analysis incorporate theories and conclusions incorporate an assessment of individuals, organizations and/or states that extends beyond self-interested satisfaction seeking motivations? (Yes / No)
Provide arguments that supports your response to Q9 (beyond self-interested agents). Plus, cite some key text from the article that supports your response.
Based on your answers to Q1-Q10, which 4PT Type (T1/T2/T3/T4) best characterizes the article? Provide a ≤120-char justification.
Do you detect any 'whack-a-mole' risk or solving T4 with T1/2/3 thinking? Cite 0–2 evidence spans.
